steps.title: Simplify the square root using the absolute value property
steps.content:
The left side has $\sqrt{(x+15)^2}$. By the property $\sqrt{a^2} = |a|$, this simplifies to $|x+15|$. The equation becomes:
$$|x+15| = x+15$$

steps.title: Determine when the absolute value equation holds
steps.content:
An absolute value equals its argument precisely when the argument is non-negative. For $|x+15| = x+15$, we need $x + 15 \geq 0$, which gives:
$$x \geq -15$$

steps.title: Find the least value
steps.content: The solution is all $x \geq -15$. The least value in this solution set is $x = -15$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
